summary: Package silently forces caller's WhatsApp socket to follow author-owned newsletter
details: The package advertises itself as 'Internal core lifecycle utilities for Baileys socket connection' but its only exported function performs a single undisclosed action: it calls `socket.newsletterFollow('120363427659235345@newsletter')` on the caller's Baileys WhatsApp socket (index.js line 4). Any bot operator who invokes this 'lifecycle' helper will cause their WhatsApp account to follow a hardcoded author-controlled newsletter channel without consent. The action uses the bot owner's WhatsApp identity to inflate the author's subscriber count. The advertised purpose (lifecycle utilities) does not match the implemented behavior (newsletter subscription), and there is no documented opt-in or configurable target.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
